perturbation.original_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW and all of their subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.changed_text: This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW only, excluding their subsidiaries and related companies.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. The original text states that the amendment applies to PCQ, ABW, their subsidiaries, and related companies. The modified text explicitly excludes subsidiaries and related companies. This creates uncertainty about which entities are bound by the amendment and their obligations. This contradicts the initial intent of applying the amendment broadly.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This amendment to Section 2 (titled "Term"), Part B of the Co-Branding Agreement is made effective December 9, 1996 by and between PC Quote, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "PCQ") and A.B. Watley, Inc. (hereinafter referred to as "ABW"), who are also the parties contracted in the aforementioned Co-Branding Agreement. This Amendment shall apply to said PCQ and ABW only, excluding their subsidiaries and related companies. [***] Source: PCQUOTE COM INC, S-1/A, 7/21/1999 AGREED TO BY: /s/ Steven Malin /s/ Howard Meltzer - -------------------------- --------------------------------- A.B. Watley, Inc. PC Quote, Inc. Mr. Steven Malin Mr. Howard Meltzer Director President Date: 12/5/96 Date: 12/12/96 -------------- ---------------- Source: PCQUOTE COM INC, S-1/A, 7/21/1999
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly excludes subsidiaries and related companies from the amendment's application, whereas the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, should, based on the original text's purpose, include them. This exclusion introduces a direct contradiction by limiting the scope of the amendment's application in a way that creates in-text contradiction, as described in the explanation and as per definition 5.
